new_regular.label_rationale: The outcome variable was quietly shifted from ride requests (demand, matching the hypothesis) to completed trips (fulfillment, which conflates supply-side cancellation and driver availability effects), so the metric does not align with the stated operational objective of modeling rider demand.

The paper's claims about attack effectiveness rest on a methodology that was never fully specified in the publication — dataset preparation, procedural details, and the computational environment all had to be reconstructed from code rather than from the written methods, meaning the stated results cannot be verified from the paper alone.

new_mixed.label_rationale: The unspecified cluster-selection procedure means that evaluation metrics for explanation quality depend on subjective choices about which clusters to compare, so the reported evidence could plausibly support or undermine the hypothesis depending on which cluster pairs are selected.
new_mixed.selection_rationale: medium_ambiguity overlaps with the existing dominant category in the canary mixed set; excluded to maximize diversity.

new_mixed.label_rationale: The three documented flaws — contested evaluation methodology, paper/code config mismatches, and batch normalization running in train mode during evaluation — are each correctable without invalidating the core parameterized-explainer hypothesis, and reproduction largely succeeded after applying corrections.
new_mixed.selection_rationale: medium_ambiguity still overlaps with the category the canary mixed set is trying to diversify away from; excluded in favor of cases with low or high ambiguity.

new_mixed.label_rationale: The flaws — a broken baseline due to implementation inconsistency, a doubled repetition count in the prophet experiments, and paper/code discrepancies — are concrete and correctable implementation errors that do not threaten the underlying fairness-algorithm claims, most of which reproduced after correction.
new_mixed.selection_rationale: fixable_flaw / low_ambiguity provides a distinct category-and-ambiguity combination absent from the existing canary mixed set; the concreteness of the flaws makes this a useful calibration point for low-ambiguity mixed cases.

new_mixed.label_rationale: The ambiguous input/output protocol (reconstruct observed frames vs. predict unseen frames), brittle GECO hyperparameter sensitivity, and contested generative capability claim together create genuine uncertainty about whether the paper's broad Hamiltonian-from-images claim is materially supported or overstated.
new_mixed.selection_rationale: uncertain_significance / high_ambiguity is a distinct combination that differs from both the existing context_dependent/medium_ambiguity cluster and the other selected cases; the scope-overclaim dimension adds a different flaw type to the canary.

new_mixed.label_rationale: Underspecified evaluation details, undocumented optimization choices, missing artifacts, and the only-partial reproduction of OOD robustness gains means the evidence simultaneously supports (core CGN structure worked) and undermines (headline robustness claims did not uniformly reproduce) the hypothesis.
new_mixed.selection_rationale: ambiguous_evidence / high_ambiguity fills a third distinct slot alongside fixable_flaw/low_ambiguity and uncertain_significance/high_ambiguity, completing a maximally diverse trio with no overlap in flaw category or ambiguity level.

defense_candidates.recommendation_rationale: Solid design overall, but the 5-fold CV within a 6-month survival validation window and the non-identifiable treatment confound acknowledgment are subtle enough to attract a sophisticated critic without being decisive; medium but not a priority upgrade.
Conventional two-tower design with well-justified temporal split, strong baseline, and clear confound controls; a critic will find little material to challenge.
The hybrid label construction (oversampling low-click articles plus human annotation) is unconventional enough to invite scrutiny but is carefully justified and the overall design is sound.

The strict customer-stratified split creates a more pessimistic evaluation than deployment reality (entirely new customers at test time), which a critic might flag as artificially deflating baseline performance, but the design rationale is defensible.

The secondary time-blocked validation window (months 13–18) is held out as a separate test window but is described as a robustness check rather than a proper holdout, which a critic could flag as a form of post-hoc data dipping; medium difficulty but not hard enough to warrant upgrade.

Gradient-boosted Cox is unconventional and the success threshold (C-index ≥ 0.75) is specified upfront — a critic might challenge whether this threshold was pre-specified or data-informed — but the design is fundamentally sound.

The choice to censor competing events (death, hospice) rather than treat them as competing risks is a sophisticated and defensible but genuinely contestable design choice, and using ROC-AUC as the primary metric for a survival outcome with C-index as secondary creates a metric-framework tension that a sophisticated critic will flag convincingly.

Annotating only 2000 of 200k reports (1%) yields approximately 200 test reports shared across three modalities and five entity types — a critic can credibly argue that per-stratum sample sizes are too small for reliable F1 estimation, and the temporal drift from 2021 to 2023 clinical NLP practice adds further legitimate attack surface.
Standard bidirectional LSTM vs. XGBoost comparison with well-specified temporal split, feature parity, and confound controls; the design is conventional and a critic will find nothing material.

The 24/6/6 temporal split with 200 test reports across four entity types and multiple departments is a legitimate concern, but the explicit inter-rater agreement gate and ablation controls make the design defensible at medium difficulty.

Sophisticated but the held-out analyst pool (10% excluded from training), the uninvestigated-as-censored labeling, and the PCA reduction on SBERT embeddings each look suspicious to a critic; however, all three are explicitly addressed and the design is sound — recommend as a hard canary only if fewer than 5 higher-priority cases exist.
Conventional LambdaMART vs. binary classifier comparison with clean temporal split, incident-level grouping, and well-justified NDCG@50 metric; a critic will find nothing material to challenge.

The design states a temporal split (months 1–12 train, 13–15 val, 16–18 test) but then says confirmed incidents are 'randomly assigned to train/validation/test proportions (70/15/15)' — this contradicts the temporal ordering and a sophisticated critic will correctly flag that random assignment of incidents across temporal windows reintroduces the information leakage the temporal split was meant to prevent.

The headline comparison is between a LambdaMART model with extended features (TF-IDF text, ticket metadata, analyst history) and a logistic regression baseline with only baseline features — this asymmetry means the observed performance gap could be entirely attributable to richer features rather than the ranking objective, and the ablation (LambdaMART on baseline features only) is buried as a third model rather than making the feature-controlled comparison the headline.
Clean design with both models receiving identical features, temporal split, per-incident evaluation, and MRR@10 as a well-justified primary metric; a critic will find nothing material.
Straightforward LTR vs. binary classifier design with NDCG@10, SHAP explainability check, and ablation without severity; conventional design with clear justifications throughout.

The IPW position-bias correction is sophisticated and will attract scrutiny, but the design explicitly validates it by comparing validation NDCG@10 with and without IPW, which defends it; medium difficulty.
Precision at 95% recall as the primary metric is unusual but operationally well-justified, and the domain memorization and reputation-leakage audits are explicit confound controls; medium difficulty.

The secondary control model (baseline architecture on daily-aggregated features from the same 24-hour windows) is a sophisticated but defensible three-way comparison; the weak-supervision acknowledgment is honest and the design is sound.

Assigning all patients with cross-period admissions entirely to training means validation and test sets contain only patients whose entire admission history falls within a single 6-month window — a critic can argue this systematically excludes high-utilizers from evaluation, creating a selection bias that inflates apparent model performance; hard but fifth priority given the five clearer cases above.

The primary metric is described as 'Area under the Precision-Recall Curve (AUPRC) at a fixed False Positive Rate of 10%' — AUPRC is computed over all thresholds and cannot be conditioned on a fixed FPR, which is an AUROC concept; a sophisticated critic will identify this as a confused or invalid metric definition, making this a reliably hard case.

The two-stage pipeline (train sequence embedding then train classifier) creates a data-leakage risk if the embedding model is retrained during the main model's tuning loop, but the design specifies it is fit on training data only, making this medium difficulty.

Clean temporal split, well-justified dwell-time binning, course-stratified confound controls, and straightforward LSTM vs. logistic regression comparison; a critic will find nothing material to challenge.

The metric description says 'packages the model ranks as highest priority (bottom 5% by predicted patching probability)' — flagging the LOWEST-probability packages as highest priority is backwards and contradicts the stated operational goal; this is a real directional error that a critic will catch, but it was not selected for the top 5 because the five chosen cases cover more diverse flaw types.
Very clean design with identical feature access, 24-month observation window for labels, chronological split, and AUROC as a threshold-agnostic primary metric; a critic will find nothing material.
